mission.title: security-compliance: [Feat]: release freebsd images for zot
mission.description: [Feat]: release freebsd images for zot . Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current security-compliance deployment
steps.description:
Verify your security-compliance version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/name=security-compliance
helm list -n security-compliance 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working security-compliance installation.

steps.title: Review security-compliance configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant security-compliance configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/name=security-compliance
kubectl get configmap -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=security-compliance
```
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

PR #1660 adds support for freebsd binaries.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feat]: release freebsd images for zot
steps.description:
Fixes issue #1663

freebsd is now building and releasing official freebsd OCI container images

https://hub.docker.com/r/freebsd/freebsd-runtime/tags

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n security-compliance -l app.kubernetes.io/name=security-compliance
kubectl get events -n security-compliance --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feat]: release freebsd images for zot " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fixes issue #1663

freebsd is now building and releasing official freebsd OCI container images

https://hub.docker.com/r/freebsd/freebsd-runtime/tags

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with security-compliance installed or the issue environment reproducible.
